data.title: U.S. government informs Nvidia it requires license to export H20 hardware to China
data.description:
Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) received a notice from the U.S. government that it needs a special license to ship its H20 graphic processing units or related hardware to China, and such a license will be required indefinitely.

Due to this development, Nvidia expects to take a charge of $5.5B in its upcoming quarterly financial results, according to a Form 8-K it filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.

Nvidia shares dropped 6% during early post-market trading on Tuesday.

"On April 9, 2025, the U.S. government, or USG, informed NVIDIA Corporation, or the Company, that the USG requires a license for export to China (including Hong Kong and Macau) and D:5 countries, or to companies headquartered or with an ultimate parent therein, of the Company's H20 integrated circuits and any other circuits achieving the H20's memory bandwidth, interconnect bandwidth, or combination thereof," the report said. "The USG indicated that the license requirement addresses the risk that the covered products may be used in, or diverted to, a supercomputer in China. On April 14, 2025, the USG informed the Company that the license requirement will be in effect for the indefinite future."

"First quarter results are expected to include up to approximately $5.5 billion of charges associated with H20 products for inventory, purchase commitments, and related reserves," Nvidia said in the filing.

Nvidia declined to comment on the issue.

Although Nvidia's more advanced artificial intelligence GPUs have already been banned for export to China, the H20 chip was designed specifically to comply with export restrictions.

The H20 is the most advanced chip offered by Nvidia that was still allowed for export to China. However, industry observers and Chinese tech companies had expected the H20 to be added to the list of other banned hardware. Last week, a report surfaced that the Trump Administration was pausing the implementation of an export ban to China for Nvidia's H20 GPUs. 

Alibaba (BABA), Tencent (TCEHY)(TCTZF) and ByteDance (BDNCE) placed at least $16B in orders for Nvidia's H20 server chips in the first three months of 2025, ahead of a potential U.S. ban on the sale of the AI chip in China.

Early on Tuesday morning, U.S. President Donald Trump posted on Truth Social that Nvidia had committed $500B to build AU supercomputers in the U.S.

"This is very big and exciting news," he said. "All necessary permits will be expedited and quickly delivered to NVIDIA, as they will to all companies committing to be part of the Golden Age of America!"

data.title: Dow downgraded 2 levels by Bank of America amid risk to dividend
data.description:
Dow (NYSE:DOW) on Tuesday was downgraded two levels to Underperform from a previous investment rating of Buy among analysts at Bank of America. They warned that a combination of softening global demand, rising trade barriers and elevated feedstock costs is eroding the chemical giant’s earnings outlook and placing its dividend at risk.

Analyst Steve Byrne described the situation as a “perfect storm” for Dow (NYSE:DOW), highlighting that recent macroeconomic weakness, the onset of a global trade war and unfavorable cost dynamics have prompted a significant reset of expectations.

The downgrade follows a 19% drop in Dow’s (DOW) share price since the U.S. announced sweeping tariffs on April 2. Byrne said that despite the selloff, the stock’s valuation no longer appears compelling given the delayed recovery timeline and deteriorating financial outlook.

One of the most pressing concerns is Dow’s (DOW) $2 billion annual dividend. Based on the revised earnings and cash flow outlook, Bank of America estimates a free cash flow shortfall of $2.6 billion across 2025 and 2026, more than double its prior estimate. This puts increasing pressure on Dow’s (DOW) ability to sustain its dividend, particularly as net leverage is expected to climb to nearly three times through 2027.

Cyclicals, China exposure

Dow's (DOW) significant exposure to cyclical industries including housing, construction and automotive makes it vulnerable in a global downturn. Although roughly 30% of its revenue comes from more stable packaging markets, the company is also a major exporter of polyethylene, which accounts for 40%–50% of its sales. China, one of its largest overseas markets, is now imposing steep tariffs on U.S. polyethylene, adding another layer of uncertainty.

Bank of America acknowledged one potential offset: U.S. feedstock costs are expected to decline, driven by weaker global demand and a reduction in exports, especially of ethane to China. However, this tailwind isn’t expected to be strong enough to counteract the broader earnings pressure.

Earnings forecasts have been cut across the board, with U.S. polyethylene pricing expected to grow more modestly than previously thought and then decline later this year due to oversupply and waning demand. Bank of America now projects Dow’s (DOW) 2025 earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization at $4.77 billion, down from $5.85 billion, and its 2026 ebitda at $5.42 billion, previously $7.04 billion.

As a result, the firm reduced its 2025 enterprise value-to-ebitda valuation multiple to 7.5 times from 8 times, and lowered its price objective for Dow (DOW) to $28 from $44.

With Dow’s (DOW) higher risks and a slower earnings recovery ahead, Bank of America said investors may find better opportunities elsewhere in the chemicals sector.

data.title: Pioneer Power targets $27M-$29M revenue for 2025 with e-Boost driving growth
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Pioneer Power Solutions (PPSI) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Nathan Mazurek highlighted the transformative year for the company, underscored by the sale of its Pioneer Custom Electrical Products unit for $50 million. This transaction resulted in a $1.50 per share special dividend and a strong balance sheet with $41.6 million in cash and no debt.
  • The company's Critical Power segment, anchored by the e-Boost platform, grew significantly in 2024, reaching $22.9 million in revenue, more than double the previous year. New product innovations like e-Boost Mini, e-Boost Mobile, and e-Boost POD have expanded its market reach.
  • Mazurek emphasized the total backlog of $19.8 million at the end of 2024, a 19% increase from the prior year, which supports their confidence in achieving 2025 guidance.
  • CFO Walter Michalec reported Q4 2024 revenue of $9.8 million, a 265% year-over-year increase, with gross margins improving to 29%. Operating losses narrowed significantly, and net income from continuing operations was $759,000.

Outlook

  • Pioneer Power reaffirmed its 2025 revenue guidance of $27 million to $29 million, driven primarily by sales and rentals of e-Boost products, alongside service and maintenance revenue.
  • The company expects approximately $17 million in revenue from equipment sales and rentals, including $2.5 million from long-term lease agreements, and $10 million from service and maintenance agreements.
  • New products, such as the HOMe-Boost targeting residential and light commercial markets, are set to launch later in 2025, adding potential revenue streams.

Financial Results

  • Fourth-quarter revenue from continuing operations was $9.8 million, up from $2.7 million in Q4 2023, driven by growth in the e-Boost business.
  • Gross profit for Q4 2024 was $2.8 million, with a gross margin of 29%, compared to 23% in the prior year.
  • Full-year revenue from continuing operations reached $22.9 million, a 106% increase year-over-year, with gross profit improving to $5.5 million and a gross margin of 24%.
  • The company's cash position was $41.6 million at the end of 2024, bolstered by the sale of the PSEP unit.

Q&A

  • Rob Brown, Lake Street Capital Markets, asked about the strong backlog and growth in e-Boost demand. CEO Mazurek attributed growth to government and quasi-government orders, including transit and school buses.
  • Sameer Joshi, H.C. Wainwright, inquired about gross margins and revenue cadence. Mazurek explained that Q4 margins benefited from a favorable mix of product and service revenue, and projected consistent quarterly revenue of $6 million to $8 million in 2025.
  • Questions also focused on the HOMe-Boost product, with Mazurek detailing its redesign to meet high-end residential and light commercial needs, targeting launch later in 2025.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts raised questions about demand sustainability, margins, and production capacity, reflecting cautious optimism. Their tone was largely neutral, with a focus on understanding the company's strategic direction and growth potential.
  • Management maintained a confident tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A, emphasizing strong backlog, product innovation, and financial discipline.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone remained consistent, focusing on the long-term growth potential of the e-Boost platform and new product launches.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue guidance for 2025 remained unchanged at $27 million to $29 million, consistent with Q3 projections.
  • The backlog decreased from $24 million in Q3 to $19.8 million in Q4, reflecting order deliveries.
  • Management's confidence in the e-Boost platform and new product launches was reiterated, with a continued emphasis on government and municipal demand.
  • Analysts' focus shifted more towards product-specific growth and margin sustainability in the current quarter.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified macroeconomic uncertainties, including potential impacts of tariffs and economic slowdown, as risks already factored into guidance.
  • Analysts expressed interest in margin consistency and the execution of new product launches like HOMe-Boost.
  • Production capacity and reliance on subcontractors were discussed as operational considerations.

Final Takeaway

Pioneer Power Solutions concluded 2024 with robust growth in its Critical Power segment, particularly through the e-Boost platform. With strong financials post-divestiture and clear revenue guidance for 2025, the company is focused on expanding its product portfolio and addressing growing market demand for mobile and off-grid EV charging solutions. Management's confidence in achieving these goals is supported by a healthy backlog and strategic product innovations.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Gold resumes gains as tariff uncertainty boosts safe-haven demand while weakening dollar
data.description:
Gold prices resumed their upward march Tuesday after a one-day drop, helped by safe-haven demand as President Trump's on-again, off-again tariff announcements continue to keep investors on edge, while an overall weaker dollar also contributed support.

The U.S. Commerce Department said Monday it had launched probes into the national security impact of semiconductor and pharmaceutical imports, a precursor to imposing tariffs, and the U.S. dollar neared a three-year low against its rivals, making gold more attractive for other currency holders.

The exemption of some electronics products from Trump's "reciprocal tariffs" provided some relief to the stock market that was quickly reversed by the threat the products could be subject to sectoral tariffs, Commerzbank analysts said.

The uncertainty both boosted gold's safe-haven demand and weakened the dollar as an alternative safe asset, further enhancing gold's appeal, according to Commerzbank, adding the European Central Bank is generally expected to cut interest rates this week and likely will consider further easing given U.S. tariff uncertainty, a boon for non-interest bearing bullion.

With central banks keeping up gold purchases, a continuation of the support seen throughout 2024, gold's next leg higher is being supported by incremental purchases from the investment community, SP Angel analysts wrote.

ETF holdings have climbed again to 89.1M oz on Tuesday from 88.7M oz on Monday, although this remains well below highs in 2020 and 2022, SP Angel said; as such, ETF demand has room to grow further.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for April delivery closed +0.4% to $3,218.70/oz, the second highest settlement in history and the metal's fifth gain in the past six sessions, while front-month April silver (XAGUSD:CUR) posted its seventh straight daily gain, also +0.4% to $32.227/oz.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (NYSEARCA:GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: Kingsmen Resources announces $1.5 million private placement
data.description:
• Kingsmen Resources (TSXV:KNG:CA) announced on Tuesday that the company has arranged a non-brokered private placement financing of up to 2.1 million units, at $0.72 per unit, to raise up to $1.51 million.
  • Each unit will consist of one common share and one-half of a common share purchase warrant. Each whole warrant will entitle the holder to purchase an additional common share at a price of $0.89 for a period of two years from closing.
  • Finder's fees may be payable and net proceeds will be used to further advance its Las Coloradas Silver/Gold project in Mexico and for working capital, said the company.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: PPG Industries downgraded at Bank of America on inflation risks
data.description:
PPG Industries (NYSE:PPG) on Tuesday was downgraded to Neutral from a previous investment rating of Buy among analysts at Bank of America. They said the maker of paints and coatings faces rising raw material costs, softening demand in key end markets and increased macroeconomic risks tied to tariffs and recessionary pressures.

While overall inflation for coatings raw materials remains modest for 2025 mostly because of declining oil prices, certain inputs such as tin plate and epoxy resin are seeing price spikes of as much as 20% from early-year levels, Steve Byrne, analyst at Bank of America, said in an April 15 report.

Inflation is projected to accelerate further in 2026, compounded by recent trade measures.

Peer commentary

Byrne pointed to recent commentary by another maker of paints and coatings, RPM International (RPM), that rising prices are a concern. The company said raw material inflation could climb into the mid-single-digit range for its 2026 fiscal year, with tariffs alone driving a 3% cost headwind.

While PPG’s (PPG_ housing exposure has been trimmed following the sale of its U.S. and Canadian architectural paint operations, the company remains vulnerable through its Mexico-based Comex brand, Byrne said. If tariffs weigh on the Mexican economy, Comex could see demand weaken.

Further risks lie in PPG’s (NYSE:PPG) automotive and refinish segments, both of which are sensitive to consumer confidence and could see volume declines in a broader economic slowdown. As inflation and macro uncertainty mount, Bank of America revised its earnings estimates for PPG (NYSE:PPG), lowering first-quarter 2025 earnings to $1.77 from $1.80 a share, and full-year EPS to $7.64 from $8.30 a share.

Shares of PPG (PPG) and competitor Axalta Coatings (AXTA) have each dropped nearly 20% since their February earnings reports and are down about 9% since the April 2 announcement of new U.S. tariffs. Axalta (AXTA), typically valued at a premium to PPG (PPG), now trades at a discount for the first time in two years. Byrne sees this valuation shift as unjustified, particularly given Axalta’s (AXTA) success with its “A-Plan” cost and margin improvement strategy.

Bank of America lowered its price target for PPG (PPG) to $126 from $143 a share, reflecting both a revised discounted cash flow model, with a higher 8.1% weighted average cost of capital and a reduced valuation multiple of 14 time 2025 earnings, down from 15.5 times, amid compression in sector peers.

While PPG (PPG) still has some upside to the new price target, Byrne said better opportunities may be found in other specialty chemicals stocks with more resilient end-market exposure and clearer margin trajectories.

data.title: U.S. crude stockpiles rose 2.4M barrels last week, API says
data.description:
The American Petroleum Institute reportedly shows a build of 2.4M barrels of oil in U.S. commercial stockpiles for the week ending April 11.

Gasoline inventories reportedly show a draw of 3M barrels for the week, distillate inventories show a draw of 3.2M barrels, and stockpiles at the Cushing storage hub show a build of 349K barrels.

The Energy Information Administration will release its weekly U.S. petroleum supply report on Wednesday.

WTI May crude oil (CL1:COM) recently traded at $61.53/bbl after settling Tuesday at $61.33/bbl.

ETF: (NYSEARCA:USO)

data.title: OFS Credit lowers estimate for NAV per share in March
data.description:
OFS Credit Company (NASDAQ:OCCI) said Tuesday it lowered its estimate for net asset value per share to $6.57-$6.67 as of March 31, 2025, from the prior estimate of $6.75-$6.85 at Feb. 28, 2025. 

The preliminary March estimate did not undergo the company’s typical quarter-end financial closing procedures.

OCCI shares gained 1.2% in after-hours trading.

data.title: Lithia & Driveway announces COO transition
data.description:
• Lithia & Driveway (NYSE:LAD) has announced that Adam Chamberlain will transition from his current role as Executive Vice President and COO effective June 1, 2025, to join Mercedes-Benz as CEO of Mercedes-Benz USA.
  • "His contributions have helped strengthen our operations, sharpen our offerings and customer awareness, drive growth, and advance our strategy. This is the beginning of a new chapter in our long-standing partnership with Mercedes-Benz, and Adam remains committed to our shared success." said Bryan DeBoer, President and CEO of Lithia & Driveway.

data.title: Paul Mueller Company further expands components products facility
data.description:
• Paul Mueller Company (OTCPK:MUEL) on Tuesday announced a $17.9M expansion to its components products facility which will increase the capacity for producing tank heads, cones, and shells.
  • Source: Press Release
